title: Milankovitch Cycles Explained
script_summary: This video explores how Earth's orbital changes influence ice ages through Milankovitch cycles.

segments.voiceover: Did you know slow shifts in Earth's orbit can trigger ice ages?
These shifts, known as Milankovitch cycles, happen over tens of thousands of years.
Earth's tilt, orbit shape, and wobble all play a role in our climate changes.
Research shows these cycles correlate with historical ice ages.
Understanding them helps us grasp our planet's climate history.
Want to learn more about our planet? Hit that follow button!

full_voiceover: Did you know slow shifts in Earth's orbit can trigger ice ages? These shifts, known as Milankovitch cycles, happen over tens of thousands of years. Earth's tilt, orbit shape, and wobble all play a role in our climate changes. Research shows these cycles correlate with historical ice ages. Understanding them helps us grasp our planet's climate history. Want to learn more about our planet? Hit that follow button!
